entry.title: What’s New in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.4 — Feb 13, 2024
entry.content:
SAP has released the version 2024.4 of SAP Datasphere on Feb 13, 2024. Your SAP Datasphere tenants have been updated probably even before Feb 13, 2024.

Administration


Photo by Vincent van Zalinge on Unsplash

1 The new monitoring view SPACE_USERS_V_EXT is deployed in the DWC_GLOBAL schema and tracks information about the user types. Monitoring Tasks, Logs and Schedules With Dedicated Monitoring Views

There are 3 fields in SPACE_USERS_V_EXT view in DWC_GLOBAL.

  • SPACE_ID Identifier of the SAP Datasphere space. Note that one space can contain several users.
  • USER_NAME Identifier of the user.
  • USER_TYPE Type of user, such as space technical user (for example database user for open SQL schemas) or global user.

 

Data Integration


Photo by Jordi Rubies on Unsplash

1 The Remote Queries monitor have been enhanced with additional columns, which provide additional information regarding used sources and runtimes. Monitoring Remote Queries

The Remote Queries monitor only shows the last 100 statements and provides many columns like Connection (Business Name or Technical Name), Start Time, End Time, Remote Statement Duration, Rows, Status, etc.

2 When you persist view data that is coming from different spaces, you can display the information of the data provenance by spaces clicking in the View Details in the log. From this View Details, you can navigate to the Remote Queries monitor and check the statement details.

The persisting views is a powerful feature, though it needs to be used carefully and efficiently. It’s a matter of great data modeling / design! “Data coming from different spaces” means that we are using shared objects from various spaces in the views and then we are persisting. So View Details and Remote Queries will show us how all these coming together. I’d like to try it sometime later.

3 You can change the run mode for a transformation flow and choose between performance and memory consumption. Change the Run Mode for a Transformation Flow

Performance vs Memory. It’s a never-ending dilemma! Let’s be clear! It’s a having or seeming to have no end and usually undesirable or unpleasant choice! Choosing one over other meaning pros & cons, gaining & sacrifice, benefit & cost! Technically speaking, right, memory or disk!

  • Performance-Optimized (Recommended) will provides SQL queries are processed directly in-memory. No intermediate result is stored on the disk to ensure a quicker data access and data consumption.
  • Memory-Optimized choice uses an enhancement of the SAP HANA Execution Engine which stores intermediate results of SQL statements on the disk.

One more thing about Transformation Flows that are great additions like we remember our Transformation Flows in BW world! But it must be pointed out that you’ve got to use the Transformations Flows carefully and resourcefully. Don’t overuse them! Don’t move data from here and there!

4 From the Views monitor, in the details screen of a data persistence view run, you can change the run mode to choose between performance and memory consumption depending on your needs. Data Persistence and Processing Mode

It’s same about persisting views. You run the view and get the result but where you are going to save the result. Here two choices: Performance-Optimized (Recommended) and Memory-Optimized. Make sure memory comes with a cost, and you need a enough disk space if you go with the second choice. Monitor Disk Volume comes in handy.

 

Data Modeling


Photo by Derek Otway on Unsplash

1 In addition to Remote Table Replication, View Persistency, Intelligent Lookup, Data Flow, and Transformation Flow tasks, you can now also nest other task chains within a task chain.

“nest other task chains within a task chain” sounds very great! You can do many things with task chains. Group multiple tasks into a task chain and run them manually once, or periodically, through a schedule. Create linear task chains in which one task is run after another. Nest other task chains within a task chain. Create task chains in which individual tasks are run in parallel and successful continuation of the entire task chain run. It’s all up to your creativity and designer / architect mindset!

2 You can now preview data for ABAP CDS views and SAP Landscape Transformation Replication Server objects if your system meets the requirements, Creating a Data Flow:

  • For CDS views, the source connection is SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2302, SAP S/4HANA on-premise 1909 or higher.
  • For SAP Landscape Transformation Replication Server objects, ABAP Add-on DMIS 2018 SP08 / DMIS 2020 SP04 is installed.

You can click to preview data of the selected operator then the Data Preview section is displayed. You can’t perform data preview on the transformation operators. That’s very handy feature during the data flow developments.

3 You can define default values for each remote table parameter. Review and Edit Imported Table Properties

If your remote table consumes parameterized HANA SQL views the parameters are displayed with their properties. You can define a default value for the parameters. That’s great! You should have always default option for the parameters to avoid overwhelming of the parameters.

 

Data Privacy and Protection


Photo by Landon Martin on Unsplash

1 You can now create data access controls in which criteria are defined as operator and value pairs. Each user can only see the records that fulfill the operator-value pairs she is authorized for in the permissions entity, including support for complex AND and OR combinations. Create an “Operator and Values” Data Access Control

It must be a great relief to be able to use operators like AND and OR combinations and all operators like EQ, NE, GT, LT, CP, etc.

2 You can now create data access controls in which criteria are defined as hierarchy values. Each user can only see the records that match the hierarchy values she is authorized for in the permissions entity, along with any of their descendants. Create a “Hierarchy” Data Access Control

I can’t imagine the benefits of this new feature in especially Finance but also other lines of businesses.

 

References

  1. What’s New in SAP Datasphere. February 13, 2024. Version 2024.4
  2. What’s New in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.4 — Feb 13, 2024 at medium.com/@tncykarc

entry.title: As of Feb 19, 2024, the SAP Datasphere Content Documentation has moved to the SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
While checking some info on SAP Datasphere Business Content document, here I’ve got this warning today.

 

SAP Datasphere Business Content

Here is the new link on SAP Help — SAP Datasphere Business Content I think it’s a great move. It’s good to have SAP Help site instead of dealing of frequently updated PDF document.

SAP has been adding great standard / business contents on SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. These business contents can be leveraged to start with your modeling in SAP Datasphere. They very advantageous to explore seeing what out there and what approaches can be applied in terms of design principles, etc.

I’ve imported and worked on SAP Ariba: Spend Analysis, Finance foundation package for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

SAP Datasphere > Semantic Onboarding > Business Content

Let’s summarize what we have so far in SAP Datasphere Business Content:

LOB (Line of Business)

  • SAP Ariba: Enterprise Analytics for Procurement — released on Feb 22, 2021
  • SAP Ariba: Spend Analysis — released on May 31, 2021, updated on Feb 20, 2023
  • SAP Business Networks — Value Analytics — released on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 15, 2022
  • Enterprise Asset Management: Plant Maintenance Analysis for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud — released on Aug 21, 2023, updated on Feb 19, 2024
  • Finance foundation package for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud — released on Aug 21, 2023
  • Sales and Distribution: Sales Analysis for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud — released on Feb 21, 2022, updated on May 21, 2022, updated on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 13, 2023
  • Service Analysis for SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Datasphere) — released on Nov 15, 2022, updated on Aug 21, 2023
  • SAP Signavio Process Insights — released on Aug 21, 2023, updated on Nov 13, 2023, updated on Feb 19, 2024
  • Solution Business (SOL): Solution Order Analysis — released on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Aug 21, 2023
  • Statistical Process Control (SPC) — released on Feb 22, 2021
  • Working Capital Dashboard for SAP S/4HANA — released on Aug 21, 2023

Industries

  • Automotive (AUT): Responsive Supply Network, Sales and Inventory — released on Nov 15, 2020, updated on May 21, 2022, updated on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 13, 2023
  • Consumer Products (CP): Revenue Growth Management — Foundation — released on Nov 15, 2020, updated on May 21, 2022, updated on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 13, 2023
  • SAP Intelligent Real Estate — released on Feb 21, 2022, updated on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 15, 2022
  • Quality Management — released on Nov 13, 2023, updated on Feb 19, 2024
  • POS Analysis for Retail — released on May 31, 2021
  • Supply Chain Management: Life Sciences Dashboard (SCM-LS) — released on May 31, 2021, updated on Aug 22, 2022, updated on Nov 13, 2023
  • Telecommunication: Customer Value Management (CVM) — released on Feb 22, 2021
  • Utilities — Meter To Cash — released on Nov 15, 2020

Cross Applications

  • Financial Analytics Dashboard for SAP Analytics Cloud — released on Nov 15, 2020, updated on May 31, 2021
  • SAP Monitoring Content (Data Integration Tasks) — updated on Mar 8, 2023
  • SAP Sustainability Control Tower Integration — released on Aug 7, 2023, updated on Feb 19, 2024

entry.title: SAP Datasphere — Don’t Create Your Spaces with These Technical Names
entry.content:
While working on SAP Datasphere one of the first tasks is creating spaces. More importantly how you organize the spaces, what’s your strategy and what best practices you may apply, etc. That’s another topic that can be dealt with in details later. Today I would like to tell you “Don’t Create Your Spaces with These Technical Names”

 

Photo by Benjamin Voros on Unsplash

Let’s start with official definition: Spaces provide a way to partition your SAP Datasphere tenant into independent virtual work environments for departments, LOB’s, data domains, project teams, and individuals.

There are technical names and business names. Technical names are permanent that cannot be changed, business names are just descriptions; change them whenever you’d like. Here we are talking about the technical names of the SAP Datasphere Spaces.

There are rules for technical names, not only for the spaces but also for all other objects in SAP Datasphere. The rules and restrictions apply to the technical names of objects that you create in SAP Datasphere.

 


SAP Datasphere > Space Management

Space Technical Names

  • The maximum length is 20 characters.
  • The space ID aka Technical Name can only contain uppercase letters, numbers, and underscores (_).

Don’t Create Your Spaces with These Technical Names

  • SYS, CREATE, or SYSTEM
  • PUBLIC, DBADMIN, MONITORING, PAL_STEM_TFIDF, SAP_PA_APL, DWC_USER_OWNER, DWC_TENANT_OWNER, DWC_AUDIT_READER, DWC_GLOBAL, and DWC_GLOBAL_LOG.
  • Also, the keywords that are reserved for the SAP HANA database cannot be used in a space ID. Check SELECT * FROM RESERVED_KEYWORDS ORDER BY RESERVED_KEYWORD;
  • ✔️SAP_CONTENT and SAP_ADMIN — for standard business contents, though you need to create them if you want to import standard business contents. 
  • ✔️ BWBRIDGE or BWBRIDGESPACE — I think though there is no specific name reserved for BW Bridge. These can be use for it.

Don’t Use These Prefixes for Your Spaces’ Technical Names

  • SAP_*
  • DWC_*
  • SYS_*

Good examples for Your Spaces’ Technical Names

  • ADMINISTRATION
  • SYSTEM_ADMIN
  • SECURITY
  • FINANCE
  • SALES
  • SUPPLY or SUPPLY_CHAIN
  • HR or HUMAN_RESOURCES
  • MANUFACTURING or PRODUCTION
  • ASSET_MANAGEMENT

References

  1. SAP Datasphere > Administering SAP Datasphere > Creating Spaces and Allocating Storage > Rules for Technical Names
  2. SAP HANA Platform > SAP HANA SQL Reference Guide for SAP HANA Platform > SQL Reference > Reserved Word > SAP HANA SQL Reference Guide for SAP HANA Platform2.0 SPS 07 > Reserved Words
  3. SAP Datasphere > Integrating Data and Managing Spaces in SAP Datasphere > Managing Your Space Integrating Data and Managing Spaces in SAP Datasphere > Managing Your Space
  4. SAP Help > Getting Started With SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge
  5. SAP > SAP Datasphere — First Guidance: Development Guidelines and Naming Conventions
  6. SAP Datasphere — Don’t Create Your Spaces with These Technical Names at medium.com/@tncykarc

entry.title: Top 3 Reasons to Attend SAP Data Unleashed 2024
entry.content:
SAP Data Unleashed is back! Tune in on March 6, 2024, at 8:00AM PST/11:00AM EST to explore the future of data, AI, and planning.  

  • Check out the top reasons to attend SAP Data Unleashed 2024:   

1) Hear directly from organizations benefiting from a business data fabric  
  
Executives from Hugo Boss and Bosch will share the benefits and learnings they’ve experienced with SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud. And you’ll hear real-world scenarios on how they are using a business data fabric to delivering meaningful data to every data consumer—with business context and logic intact.   

2) Discover new data management and generative AI capabilities 

Our SAP executive board members Julia White and Juergen Mueller will announce the latest innovations around data, AI, and planning. You’ll also hear from Dominik Asam, Chief Financial Officer and executive board member at SAP, on transforming Financial Planning & Analysis by implementing the latest innovations around data and AI.   

3) Watch product demos & participate in live Q&A 
 
See the business data fabric in action at SAP Data Unleashed! We will highlight the latest innovations in our product portfolio from data, AI and planning. You will also have the opportunity to get all your questions answered by our team of experts with live chat Q&A.  
 
Register for SAP Data Unleashed  

What are you waiting for? Don't miss out on the chance to learn about the latest innovations and see them in action. Sign up today for SAP Data Unleashed to be at the forefront of data innovation!

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.5 — Feb 27, 2024
entry.content:
There are 1 Administration and 6 SAP BW Bridge enhancements in version 2024.5 of SAP Datasphere. Since there is no Data Integration enhancements in this release. I hope it won’t require any more SAP Notes, as such updates in your data sources, especially SAP S/4HANA, etc.

 

Administration

Photo by Barrett Ward on Unsplash

1 Users with the DW Administrator global role can use the new Authorization Overview page to see all the users, roles, and spaces in the tenant and how they relate to each other. You can filter by user, role, or space to see, for example, which users are assigned with which roles to a particular space. View Authorizations (Users, Roles and Spaces)

It’s great addition. Try Security > Authorization Overview. You can see all all users and roles and filter on User Names and Roles Names. It’s more like analytical report. But wait a minute, there is not navigation option to the user or the roles. It would be great if we are able to click and jump the Users and the Roles maintenance.

 

SAP BW Bridge

Photo by Iván Díaz on Unsplash

1 In the Me Area in the SAP BW bridge cockpit, you can make launchpad settings. The SAP Fiori launchpad displays notifications from the Request Management and Process Chains areas that are relevant for you due to your business role. Notifications

Me Area > Settings, you can see all the notification types that you have ever received. You can specify which content you want to see in the notifications area and you can swich off and on every notification type. You can also choose whether to display a preview of the notifications in the area above the current screen.

2 You can use various APIs to load, activate, and delete data. APIs for InfoObjects and DataStore Objects

It sounds like a very neat addition that you can use APIs for Master Data, APIs for Master Data, APIs for DataStore Objects. The example codes of API usages are more like ABAP code. I’m not sure exactly where we write / use this code. Is it in onpremise BW systems, or where!

3 A process chain for streaming can be started and executed very frequently, and makes it possible to update data in real-time to DataStore objects. Process chains therefore provide a holistic solution for periodic and very frequent scheduling and for streaming. You set whether or not a process chain should be used for streaming using an attribute in the process chain. Process Chains for Streaming

A process chain for streaming can be started and executed very frequently, and makes it possible to update data in real-time to DataStore objects. Okay, that sounds awesome. If you execute the process chain in streaming mode, it seems to be that BW Bridge provides more resources like “worker” jobs to complete the process fast.

4 If you create an InfoObject or a DataStore object and select the package ZLOCAL or a subordinate package, the system issues a warning as objects in the package ZLOCAL cannot be transported.

It’s simple and necessary. The communication is important. As you know all from local packages are not for transports! That’s it!

5 Data Tiering allows you to assign data to various storage areas and storage media. The critera for this decision are data type, operational considerations, performance requirements, frequency of access, and security requirements. Data Tiering Optimization (DTO) helps you to classify the data in your DataStore object as warm or cold, depending on how frequently it is accessed. Data Tiering Optimization (DTO)

You can classify the data in your DataStore object as warm or cold, depending on how frequently it is accessed. The warm means the data is stored in an SAP HANA extension node or managed with SAP HANA Native Storage Extension. The cold means the data is stored externally in SAP IQ.

6 Using the role SAP_BR_DEVELOPER_DWC, you can now extract metadata from SAP BW bridge using the catalog in SAP Datasphere. Business Role Templates and Business Catalogs

SAP_BR_DEVELOPER_DWS in BW Bridge allows you to extract metadata from SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge through the catalog to SAP Datasphere. Let’s have metadata which is always helpful! Give me more information I won’t complain and I will appreciate.

 

References

  1. What’s New in SAP Datasphere. February 27, 2024. Version 2024.5
  2. What’s New in SAP Datasphere Version 2024.5 — Feb 27, 2024 at medium.com/@tncykarc
